channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: Phytotherapeutic potential of Gossypium barbadense L. root as an alternative therapy for colitis: a comprehensive review with network pharmacology insights.
item.description: Inflammopharmacology - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), particularly ulcerative colitis (UC), remains a major global health challenge characterized by chronic, relapsing inflammation of the colonic mucosa. Although conv...

item.title: IgA nephropathy: an overview of the disease, its pathophysiology, and involvement of the gut-kidney axis.
item.description: Kidney international supplements - Review - IgA nephropathy (IgAN) is the most common primary glomerular disease worldwide. Its incidence and prevalence vary widely, with estimates of annual incidence ranging from 0.06 to 10.5 per 100,000 pe...

item.title: qMAP decodes RNA fragmentation dynamics in development and disease.
item.description: Molecular systems biology - Noncanonical small RNAs, such as tRNA-derived (tsRNAs) and rRNA-derived (rsRNAs) fragments, are more abundant than microRNAs and arise from selective cleavage events rather than random degradation....

item.title: Roxadustat-loaded pH-sensitive Anionic Liposomes Ameliorating Experimental Colitis in Mice: Formulation, Cellular Uptake and In vivo Evaluation.
item.description: Current drug delivery - Roxadustat, an inhibitor of hypoxia-inducible factor prolyl hydroxylase, has therapeutic potential for colitis but is limited clinically by poor water solubility and rapid gastrointestinal absorpti...
